opp.ais_why:
• October discovery noted 'strong interest for AI-driven automation across sourcing, contract management, eProcurement, and invoicing,' with SF flagging ANNA negotiation and Contract Intelligence as named asks.
• Workday go-live in January is the gating event; team explicitly deferred any Zycus evaluation until post-stabilization, so interest is real but currently parked.
• Only one substantive call plus a short Feb follow-up; SharePoint resource site is the holding pattern, keeping AI signal warm but unvalidated by deeper demos.

metrics.narrative: Michael Katzeff articulated two quantified business pressures on the 31 Oct 2025 discovery call: (1) a $1 billion hospital construction project creating extraordinary procurement volume and complexity, and (2) pharmaceutical procurement optimization needs driven by cost containment and compliance in a research hospital environment. He stated the team is 'under pressure' due to these concurrent demands and that the organization has 'no dedicated procurement platform currently in place' post-Workday migration, relying on manual processes. However, no specific quantified impact was provided: no baseline spend under management, no manual-process cycle time, no error/compliance incident rate, no FTE burden, and no projected ROI or savings target. The pain is real and substantial (research hospital + $1B construction), but unquantified. The business case for CFO/board approval (which Michael stated would be required) has not been built, and without the buyer reactivating, Zycus cannot co-develop the metrics to anchor it.

champion.narrative: No champion identified. Michael Katzeff (Senior Manager of Procurement Operations, Influencer role) was the sole buyer contact on the 31 Oct 2025 discovery call. He expressed interest in Zycus capabilities (eProcurement, CLM, AI negotiation, Guided Buying) and engaged in a detailed use-case discussion, but he has not demonstrated champion behaviors: no introduction to the Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or executive sponsor; no advocacy or internal selling on Zycus's behalf; no sharing of internal decision timelines, competitive landscape, or political dynamics; and no proactive engagement to advance the evaluation. He requested Zycus resources (one-pagers, slide deck, demo videos) to share internally with the Contracting team (Director Susan Wills) and other stakeholders, but there is no evidence this occurred or resulted in multi-threading. Most critically, he has been unresponsive for 8 months (last contact 31 Oct 2025), including two rep re-engagement attempts (18 Mar 2026, 19 Jun 2026 per Next Step). His engagement status is cooling-to-dormant. He may have been a coach (provided process insight, named stakeholders), but he has not acted as a champion, and the relationship is now inactive.

competition.narrative: No competitor identified from any source. The Salesforce Competitors__c field is not populated, no competitive vendor was mentioned on the 31 Oct 2025 discovery call, and no task, next step, or field history references a rival. Michael Katzeff stated the organization has 'no dedicated procurement platform currently in place' post-Workday migration, which could indicate a greenfield opportunity (no incumbent P2P/CLM vendor to displace). However, he also mentioned that the Contracting team (Director Susan Wills) manages 'existing vendor contracts' and that procurement decisions depend on 'when those are up for renewal', which implies some supplier relationships exist, though their scope (contract management, invoicing, sourcing tools) is unknown. The 8-month dormancy and lack of buyer reactivation raise the possibility that Dana-Farber is (a) evaluating alternatives quietly without Zycus visibility, (b) has deprioritized procurement automation in favor of Workday-native or manual processes, or (c) is waiting for an internal forcing function (contract renewal, budget cycle) before initiating a formal vendor selection. For this deal, 'no known competitor' means Zycus has no competitive intelligence, not that the opportunity is sole-sourced. The gap is a knowledge and visibility problem.

identify_pain.narrative: Michael Katzeff clearly articulated operational and financial pain on 31 Oct 2025: Dana-Farber is undertaking a $1 billion hospital construction project, creating extraordinary procurement volume, supplier management complexity, and cross-departmental coordination demands (Contracting, Finance/AP, Legal). Concurrently, the organization has pharmaceutical procurement optimization needs driven by cost containment and regulatory compliance pressures in a research hospital setting. He stated the team is 'under pressure' due to these concurrent workloads and that the organization has 'no dedicated procurement platform currently in place' following the Workday migration, meaning procurement, invoicing, contract management, and supplier interactions are largely manual. The pain owner is the Procurement Operations team (Michael Katzeff, Senior Manager; mentioned colleagues Ronnie Crecco and Charles Maranga), but the pain has enterprise-wide impact (Contracting, Finance/AP, and the capital projects organization managing the $1B construction). This is confirmed, articulated pain with clear operational and financial stakes, but it has not been quantified (no baseline metrics, no cost of inaction) and, critically, the buyer has not reactivated to address it in 8 months, which raises the question of whether the pain has been deprioritized, addressed via another solution, or is being managed with existing manual/Workday processes.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified. Michael Katzeff (Senior Manager of Procurement Operations, Influencer role) stated on the 31 Oct 2025 call that budget approval would require 'higher ups' and specifically named 'CFO or board' as the approval authority. He described a multi-stakeholder decision process involving Contracting (Director Susan Wills), Legal, and Finance/AP, indicating this is a cross-departmental, executive-led decision, not an operational procurement buy. However, no executive has been engaged, no VP Supply Chain or CPO has joined any conversation, and no CFO/board member has been mapped or contacted. The evidence shows budget authority sits at the C-level (CFO) or board, but Zycus has no relationship with, visibility into, or access to that layer. Michael Katzeff has operational influence but explicitly stated he does not control the commercial decision. The 8-month dormancy and lack of buyer response to two rep re-engagement attempts (Mar and Jun 2026) further suggests no executive sponsor is driving this internally.

sources: Avoma discovery call 31 Oct 2025, Michael Katzeff
OpportunityContactRole: only Michael Katzeff (Influencer); no multi-threading, no exec participation
decision_process.narrative: Michael Katzeff described a multi-stakeholder, cross-departmental decision process on 31 Oct 2025: procurement automation would require alignment and approval from Contracting (Director Susan Wills, who manages existing vendor contracts and renewal schedules), Legal, Finance/AP (for invoicing and payment workflows), and executive approval from 'higher ups' (CFO or board). He stated that timing would depend on 'when those [existing contracts] are up for renewal and when we're on contract with those vendors', indicating procurement decisions are gated by contract cycles and budgetary windows. However, this is a second-hand description from an operational manager, not a confirmed process. No executive has articulated the decision process, no RFP timeline has been shared, no evaluation committee has been formed, and no formal approval chain has been documented. The call sequence itself shows no process: one discovery call (31 Oct 2025), then 8 months of silence. No one has joined, no one has escalated, no cross-functional stakeholders have participated. The described process may be accurate, but it has not been activated, and Zycus has no visibility into whether it will be.

decision_criteria.narrative: Michael Katzeff outlined specific use-case interest on the 31 Oct 2025 call: AI-driven procurement automation with strong interest in eProcurement, eInvoicing, Smart Invoices, Guided Buying, ANNA (AI negotiation agent), and Contract Intelligence (CLM). He emphasized the need for a 'dedicated procurement platform' to handle pharmaceutical procurement compliance and the extraordinary volume from the $1B construction project, and stated that Workday alone would not address these needs. However, this is stated interest and product-category scoping, not formalized evaluation criteria. No RFP, no weighted scorecard, no test-case validation, no technical or functional requirements document (BRD), and no Shoe Fit assessment has occurred. The buyer has not progressed to a structured evaluation stage. The criteria exist as exploratory interest anchored to real pain (construction + pharma procurement), but they are unconfirmed as the actual decision framework the organization will use to select a vendor. Without reactivation and executive involvement, these criteria may shift or be overridden when (if) a formal process begins.

deltas.subject: REACTIVATE WITH the relevant stakeholder. Buyer deferred until Workday go-live in January 2026, now 5 months past. Rep reached out 18 Mar and 19 Jun with no reply. Do not ask 'how is Workday going' generically; lead with a concrete post-Workday use case (e.g. 'now that Workday is live, other healthcare systems integrated Zycus Smart Invoices in 8 weeks and cut AP cycle time 40 percent; can we show you the Workday-native integration in a 20-minute session'). Without a value-led reactivation this week, treat this as a disqualify.

DEVELOP THE CHAMPION OR MULTI-THREAD AGGRESSIVELY. Michael Katzeff expressed interest but has taken no internal action in 8 months (no stakeholder introductions, no reply to rep outreach, no follow-up on promised SharePoint materials). Either (a) re-engage Michael with a concrete ask that proves advocacy (introduce us to the relevant stakeholder in Contracting or the VP Supply Chain this week, or share our ROI deck with them and report back), or (b) bypass and multi-thread directly to Contracting, Finance/AP, or executive leadership via the account-level contacts.
